query: Design the caching strategy for the Beads dependency graph in this harness. Propose options and recommend one.
expected_skill_contribution: The architect should propose at least 2 alternatives with a trade-off table, recommend the simplest design that meets requirements, and produce a draft ADR — baseline gives one solution without alternatives.
fixture_intent: Beads data files and the beads-harness skill are present so the architect can ground alternatives in actual data shapes and access patterns rather than pure speculation.
baseline_gaps: Proposes only one caching solution without considering alternatives or explaining why others were rejected.
Presents analysis as prose paragraphs rather than a structured trade-off table with Option/Pros/Cons/Complexity/Recommended columns.
Produces no ADR and leaves the decision without a canonical record of Status, Context, Decision, and Consequences.
expected_behavior: Proposes at least 2 distinct design alternatives for the caching strategy, not just one.
Produces a trade-off table with exactly these columns: Option / Pros / Cons / Complexity / Recommended.
Recommends the simplest design that satisfies the stated requirements, with an explicit rationale for why complexity above that threshold is not justified.
Produces a draft ADR with all required sections: Status / Context / Decision / Consequences / Date.

query: Design the agent routing mechanism for the harness: how should user intent automatically map to the right specialist agent?
expected_skill_contribution: The architect should analyse current state first, identify Red Flags in the current design, propose 2+ alternatives with trade-offs, and produce an ADR.
fixture_intent: The existing routing mechanism is embedded across agent descriptions and the orchestration skill, so the architect can identify real coupling or design smells before proposing alternatives.
baseline_gaps: Proposes a routing design without first analysing what the current mechanism does or what constraints it imposes.
Misses observable Red Flags (Magic String routing, God Object orchestrator, Tight Coupling between recipe and agent) without naming them.
Presents a single routing solution without a trade-off table or competing alternatives.
expected_behavior: Analyses existing routing architecture from recipe and skill files before proposing any new design.
Names any Red Flags found (e.g., Magic String routing, God Object, Tight Coupling) with the specific file and evidence for each.
Proposes at least 2 routing design alternatives with a trade-off table using Option / Pros / Cons / Complexity / Recommended columns.
Writes a draft ADR in canonical format with a Consequences section that lists both positive outcomes and negative trade-offs of the chosen design.

query: Review the full harness architecture after the agent roster overhaul. Identify technical debt, scalability risks, and propose the next architectural iteration.
expected_skill_contribution: The architect should produce a current-state analysis, technical debt map, scalability risk assessment, and a design proposal with trade-offs — not just a status summary.
fixture_intent: A full harness with 11 agents, multiple skill packages, and an orchestration recipe layer exists so the architect can map real patterns, identify specific debt items with file evidence, and stress-test the design against growth scenarios.
baseline_gaps: Summarises current state without identifying specific technical debt items tied to files or components.
Omits scalability risk assessment and produces no roadmap for how the design holds up under growth.
Produces no design proposal with alternatives — ends at diagnosis without a recommended next iteration.
expected_behavior: Produces a current-state analysis identifying existing patterns (routing strategy, skill packaging, recipe hierarchy) and their conventions.
Documents technical debt as a named list with specific file/component evidence for each item (not vague categories).
Identifies scalability risks and includes a scalability roadmap addressing at least three growth scenarios (e.g., 10 → 100 → 1000 agents).
Proposes the next architectural iteration with a trade-off table and a draft ADR capturing the Status, Context, Decision, Consequences, and Date.
